rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE FIRST AMEDMENT AND RESTATEMENT OF YETIVE EDMONDS 2010 TRUST AGREEMENT DATED MARCH 16, 2010
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The trustee requests fees of $52,839.14, based on 1% of the ending value of the trust assets, less $23,396.82 paid to the property management company, for his services rendered during this two-year account period. The trustee's time records show he actually provided 115.75 hours of services as trustee during the same period. The trust paid $14,467.40 to Wells Fargo during this account period for advisory fees which are not proposed to be deducted from the 1% fees. 2) The trustee requests that the bond be reduced to $2,082,000. The trustee's bond has been increased to $6,811,000 to cover the proceeds from the sales of two parcels of real property the trust received after this account period. 3) The trustee requests preliminary distribution of the net proceeds from the sale of real property the trust received after this account period to the beneficiaries. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BENJAMIN M. PICETTI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For Payment As Litigation Counsel For Temporary Conservatee
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1. Timesheets are redacted 2. Petitioner requests fees for time spent on complaint filed with the state bar. 3. Per supplement filed on 4/13/22, Petitioner requests $179,313.67 in fees and costs, less $30,000 retainer, for remaining fees and costs of $149,313.67. a. The figure does not appear to match the invoices and requests made: $171,218.97 (total invoices) - $30,000 (retainer paid) + (3hrs * $550 additional time spent on the petition, per original petition) + (2 * $550 anticipated time for prepping and attending hearing, per original petition) + (5.3 * $550 additional time to address Examiner's Notes) = $146,883.97. b. No amendment was filed to request the additional fees for 5.3 hours to address Examiner's Notes. A copy of the supplement was only sent to counsel. c. Petitioner alleges that the redacted entry for 10/27/21 was a duplicate entry and has been removed from the fee request. However, there is no showing that the entry was actually removed. There is a $1,405 difference between the total of the unredacted entries and the total invoiced amount. d. Petitioner alleges that the redacted entry for 8/10/20 was removed from the fee request. However, there is no showing that the entry was actually removed. There is a $100 difference between the total of the unredacted entries and the total invoiced amount. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.

Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD FRANCIS CONWAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition By Executor To Determine Ownership Of Estate Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/14/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain why there are no exhibits that are mentioned in the petition attached to the petition. Please file the exhibits. 2) Please file a complete copy of the trust and any and all amendments. 3) Please file copies of all deeds mentioned in the petition. 4) Please address Evidence Code 662 and the presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof. 5) Proposed order does not comply with California Rules of Court 2.108. See CRC 2.108 (1) The lines on each page must be one and one-half spaced or double-spaced and numbered consecutively. (2) Descriptions of real property may be single-spaced. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
